questions.question: Differentiate between inflection and derivation with suitable examples.
expectedPoints: Inflection does not change core meaning or part-of-speech (e.g., 'walk' -> 'walked', 'cat' -> 'cats').
Derivation creates a new word, often with a new meaning and part-of-speech (e.g., 'teach' (verb) -> 'teacher' (noun), 'happy' (adj) -> 'unhappy' (adj)).

questions.question: Explain the process of morphological parsing using Finite-State Transducers (FSTs). Illustrate with an example of your choice.
expectedPoints: Definition of FST as an automaton with input and output capabilities.
Role in analysis (surface form to lexical form + features) and generation (vice-versa).
Mention of cascading FSTs (lexical, morphological rule).
Brief mention of handling orthographic rules (e.g., insertion of 'e' in 'foxes').
